revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Terayama_Minamiyama_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Terayama_Minamiyama_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: 新しいページ：寺山南山古墳（大阪府堺市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 寺山南山古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = [[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町1丁 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 21.70 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 28|経度秒 = 48.25 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 44.7m×39.2m
高さ4.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「百舌鳥古墳群」に包含
[[世界文化遺産]]「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 寺山南山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''寺山南山古墳'''（てらやまみなみやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]（長方形墳）。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録されている（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|[[堺市博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|囲形埴輪}}{{small|堺市博物館展示。}}]] [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳|ミサンザイ古墳]]（履中天皇陵）後円部北東側の外濠に接して築造された古墳である。墳丘上に宅地が建設されて改変されているほか、[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）以降に発掘調査・地中レーダー探査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で、長辺44.7メートル・短辺39.2メートル・高さ4.7メートルを測る[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳]（堺市ホームページ）。。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表には[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められるほか、最古級の[[須恵器]]が採集されている{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。墳丘南東側には長辺9.9メートル・短辺3.4メートルの[[造出]]を有し、付近から埴輪列とともに家形埴輪・囲形埴輪が検出されている。また墳丘周囲には幅6-8.5メートルの周濠が巡らされており、周濠の一部はミサンザイ古墳と共有する。埋葬施設・副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。百舌鳥古墳群では数少ない方墳の1つであり、ミサンザイ古墳と周濠が重なることから、ミサンザイ古墳と密接な関わりを持つ古墳（[[陪塚]]）と想定される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2014年]]（平成26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）。 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片
{{small|堺市博物館展示。}} == 遺跡歴 == * [[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）、測量図の報告（当時には方墳と認識）{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）、墳丘上に宅地の建設{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）、発掘調査（堺市教育委員会）。 * [[2000年]]度（平成12年度）、発掘調査：TYM-2地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2002年]]に報告）。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、発掘調査：TYM-3地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2010年]]に報告）。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、地中レーダー探査、発掘調査：TYM-4地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]（平成26年）3月18日、既指定の古墳7基を統合し、寺山南山古墳含む古墳10基を追加指定して、指定名称を「百舌鳥古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}}。 * [[2017年]]度（平成29年度）、発掘調査：TYM-5地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に報告）。 * [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）7月6日、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 関連施設 == * [[堺市博物館]]（堺市堺区百舌鳥夕雲町） - 寺山南山古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（堺市設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=寺山南山古墳|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|volume=第7版|url=|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-2地点|title=平成13年度 国庫補助事業発掘調査報告書|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-3地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査3|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-4地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査6|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査12 -寺山南山古墳（TYM-5）発掘調査報告書-|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:てらやまみなみやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]]

[[世界文化遺産]]「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 寺山南山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''寺山南山古墳'''（てらやまみなみやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]（長方形墳）。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録されている（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|[[堺市博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|囲形埴輪}}{{small|堺市博物館展示。}}]] [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳|ミサンザイ古墳]]（履中天皇陵）後円部北東側の外濠に接して築造された古墳である。墳丘上に宅地が建設されて改変されているほか、[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）以降に発掘調査・地中レーダー探査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で、長辺44.7メートル・短辺39.2メートル・高さ4.7メートルを測る[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳]（堺市ホームページ）。。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表には[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められるほか、最古級の[[須恵器]]が採集されている{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。墳丘南東側には長辺9.9メートル・短辺3.4メートルの[[造出]]を有し、付近から埴輪列とともに家形埴輪・囲形埴輪が検出されている。また墳丘周囲には幅6-8.5メートルの周濠が巡らされており、周濠の一部はミサンザイ古墳と共有する。埋葬施設・副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。百舌鳥古墳群では数少ない方墳の1つであり、ミサンザイ古墳と周濠が重なることから、ミサンザイ古墳と密接な関わりを持つ古墳（[[陪塚]]）と想定される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2014年]]（平成26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）。 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片
{{small|堺市博物館展示。}} == 遺跡歴 == * [[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）、測量図の報告（当時には方墳と認識）{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）、墳丘上に宅地の建設{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）、発掘調査（堺市教育委員会）。 * [[2000年]]度（平成12年度）、発掘調査：TYM-2地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2002年]]に報告）。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、発掘調査：TYM-3地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2010年]]に報告）。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、地中レーダー探査、発掘調査：TYM-4地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]（平成26年）3月18日、既指定の古墳7基を統合し、寺山南山古墳含む古墳10基を追加指定して、指定名称を「百舌鳥古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}}。 * [[2017年]]度（平成29年度）、発掘調査：TYM-5地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に報告）。 * [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）7月6日、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 関連施設 == * [[堺市博物館]]（堺市堺区百舌鳥夕雲町） - 寺山南山古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（堺市設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=寺山南山古墳|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|volume=第7版|url=|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-2地点|title=平成13年度 国庫補助事業発掘調査報告書|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-3地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査3|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-4地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査6|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査12 -寺山南山古墳（TYM-5）発掘調査報告書-|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:てらやまみなみやまこふん}} [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

[[世界文化遺産]]「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 寺山南山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''寺山南山古墳'''（てらやまみなみやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]（長方形墳）。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録されている（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|[[堺市博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|囲形埴輪}}{{small|堺市博物館展示。}}]] [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳|ミサンザイ古墳]]（履中天皇陵）後円部北東側の外濠に接して築造された古墳である。墳丘上に宅地が建設されて改変されているほか、[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）以降に発掘調査・地中レーダー探査が実施されている。 墳形は方形（長方形）で、長辺44.7メートル・短辺39.2メートル・高さ4.7メートルを測る[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳]（堺市ホームページ）。。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表には[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められるほか、最古級の[[須恵器]]が採集されている{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。墳丘南東側には長辺9.9メートル・短辺3.4メートルの[[造出]]を有し、付近から埴輪列とともに家形埴輪・囲形埴輪が検出されている。また墳丘周囲には幅6-8.5メートルの周濠が巡らされており、周濠の一部はミサンザイ古墳と共有する。埋葬施設・副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。百舌鳥古墳群では数少ない方墳の1つであり、ミサンザイ古墳と周濠が重なることから、ミサンザイ古墳と密接な関わりを持つ古墳（[[陪塚]]）と想定される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2014年]]（平成26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）。 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片
{{small|堺市博物館展示。}} == 遺跡歴 == * [[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）、測量図の報告（当時には方墳と認識）{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）、墳丘上に宅地の建設{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）、発掘調査（堺市教育委員会）。 * [[2000年]]度（平成12年度）、発掘調査：TYM-2地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2002年]]に報告）。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、発掘調査：TYM-3地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2010年]]に報告）。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、地中レーダー探査、発掘調査：TYM-4地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]（平成26年）3月18日、既指定の古墳7基を統合し、寺山南山古墳含む古墳10基を追加指定して、指定名称を「百舌鳥古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}}。 * [[2017年]]度（平成29年度）、発掘調査：TYM-5地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に報告）。 * [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）7月6日、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 関連施設 == * [[堺市博物館]]（堺市堺区百舌鳥夕雲町） - 寺山南山古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（堺市設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=寺山南山古墳|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|version=第7版|url=|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-2地点|title=平成13年度 国庫補助事業発掘調査報告書|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-3地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査3|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-4地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査6|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査12 -寺山南山古墳（TYM-5）発掘調査報告書-|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:てらやまみなみやまこふん}} [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * **Sakai City Museum** (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * **Sakai City Museum** (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. {{Infobox Ancient Tomb Japan |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City {{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

{{Infobox ancient site |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue ware fragments

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
