{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |artifacts= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alias= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial_facility= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map2= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - revision.comment: another mass replace to make the parameters work correctly
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= |part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |Burial Facility= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World Cultural Heritage (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Cultural Heritage site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Cultural Heritage of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

Height 4.7 m |Burial Facility= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun part_of) |designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun part_of|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun part_of and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun part_of" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun part_of National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. Height 4.7 m |Burial Facility= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Cultural {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |Burial Facility= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designated as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designated as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays artifacts from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |construction_date= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |designation1= National other_designation (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |icon= Square Kofun |label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |label_position= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, designation1 as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |other_designation1= National other_other_designation (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |other_designation2= UNESCO World other_other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |other_designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |other_designation1= National other_designation (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |other_designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |other_designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama
