{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |other_designation1= National other_designation (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |other_designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |other_designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National other_designation {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "other_designation of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National other_designation Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |other_designation1= National other_designation (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |other_designation2= UNESCO World other_designation (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |other_designation3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, other_designation1 as a National other_designation and inscribed as a UNESCO World other_designation site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National other_designation in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}}

Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}}

Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{citation needed|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

"Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{citation needed|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国指定文化財等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{citation needed|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 寺山南山古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = [[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町1丁 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 21.70 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 28|経度秒 = 48.25 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 44.7m×39.2m
高さ4.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[須恵器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「百舌鳥古墳群」に包含
[[世界文化遺産]]「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 寺山南山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''寺山南山古墳'''（てらやまみなみやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]上野芝町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]（長方形墳）。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録されている（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形埴輪}}{{small|[[堺市博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|囲形埴輪}}{{small|堺市博物館展示。}}]] [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳|ミサンザイ古墳]]（履中天皇陵）後円部北東側の外濠に接して築造された古墳である。墳丘上に宅地が建設されて改変されているほか、[[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）以降に発掘調査・地中レーダー探査が実施されている。 墳形は方形（長方形）で、長辺44.7メートル・短辺39.2メートル・高さ4.7メートルを測る[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳]（堺市ホームページ）。。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表には[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められるほか、最古級の[[須恵器]]が採集されている{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。墳丘南東側には長辺9.9メートル・短辺3.4メートルの[[造出]]を有し、付近から埴輪列とともに家形埴輪・囲形埴輪が検出されている。また墳丘周囲には幅6-8.5メートルの周濠が巡らされており、周濠の一部はミサンザイ古墳と共有する。埋葬施設・副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。百舌鳥古墳群では数少ない方墳の1つであり、ミサンザイ古墳と周濠が重なることから、ミサンザイ古墳と密接な関わりを持つ古墳（[[陪塚]]）と想定される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2014年]]（平成26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「百舌鳥古墳群」のうち）。 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片
{{small|堺市博物館展示。}} == 遺跡歴 == * [[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）、測量図の報告（当時には方墳と認識）{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）、墳丘上に宅地の建設{{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]度（[[平成]]11年度）、発掘調査（堺市教育委員会）。 * [[2000年]]度（平成12年度）、発掘調査：TYM-2地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2002年]]に報告）。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、発掘調査：TYM-3地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2010年]]に報告）。 * [[2010年]]度（平成22年度）、地中レーダー探査、発掘調査：TYM-4地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年]]（平成26年）3月18日、既指定の古墳7基を統合し、寺山南山古墳含む古墳10基を追加指定して、指定名称を「百舌鳥古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}}。 * [[2017年]]度（平成29年度）、発掘調査：TYM-5地点（堺市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に報告）。 * [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）7月6日、[[ユネスコ]]の[[世界文化遺産]]に登録（「[[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 関連施設 == * [[堺市博物館]]（堺市堺区百舌鳥夕雲町） - 寺山南山古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（堺市設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=寺山南山古墳|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|version=第7版|url=|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-2地点|title=平成13年度 国庫補助事業発掘調査報告書|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-3地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査3|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=寺山南山古墳 TYM-4地点|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査6|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査12 -寺山南山古墳（TYM-5）発掘調査報告書-|volume=|url=|publisher=堺市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1797|百舌鳥古墳群}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html 寺山南山古墳] - 堺市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:てらやまみなみやまこふん}} [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture |coordinates= {{coord|34|33|21.70|N|135|28|48.25|E}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early [[5th century]] |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|西区 (堺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early [[5th century]]. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments

{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * [[1926]] (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1961]] (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * [[1999]] (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * [[2000]] (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * [[2008]] (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * [[2010]] (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * [[2014]] (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * [[2017]] (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * [[2019]] (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]] [[ja:寺山南山古墳]]
