Kofun |map_dot_label= top |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961}} (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999}} (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008}} (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}} (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017}} (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}} (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{Commons category|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q66124365}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961}} (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999}} (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008}} (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014}} (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017}} (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}} (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{Commons category|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q66124365}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[ja:寺山南山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[ja:寺山南山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun]][[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5561|N|135.48|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}} (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{Commons category|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q66124365}} [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}} (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寺山南山古墳]] {{Commons category|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q66124365}} [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name= Terayama Minamiyama Kofun |image= |alternate_name= | part_of= {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location= {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.5561|N|135.48|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type= {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size= 44.7 m × 39.2 m
Height 4.7 m |burial= Unknown |burial goods= {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |satellite_tombs= |built= Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |buried_person= |imperial_tomb= |Historic Site1= National Historic Site (part of Mozu Kofun Group) |Historic Site2= UNESCO World Historic Site (part of {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}) |Historic Site3= |notable_features= |map= Japan Osaka |type= Square Kofun |map_dot_label= Terayama Minamiyama
Kofun |map_dot_label= top |architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} }} '''Terayama Minamiyama Kofun''' (寺山南山古墳) is a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site (within the {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}). == Overview == [[File:寺山南山古墳 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] [[File:寺山南山古墳 囲形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Enclosure-shaped Haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}}]] The kofun is located northeast of the outer moat of the {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}}). While the mound has been modified by residential development, excavations and ground-penetrating radar surveys have been conducted since 1999. The mound is rectangular, measuring 44.7 meters by 39.2 meters with a height of 4.7 meters, and is constructed in two tiers. Features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and a cylindrical haniwa row. Some of the oldest known {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments were recovered from the site. A protrusion on the southeast side of the mound, measuring 9.9 meters by 3.4 meters, revealed house-shaped and enclosure-shaped haniwa alongside haniwa rows. Surrounding the mound is a moat, 6–8.5 meters wide, partially shared with the Misanzai Kofun. The burial facility and grave goods remain unidentified, but the kofun is dated to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. It is one of the few square kofun in the Mozu Kofun Group and is considered closely associated with the Misanzai Kofun, likely serving as a satellite tomb. The kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the "Mozu Kofun Group" National Historic Site in 2014. 寺山南山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪・須恵器片.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, and Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at Sakai City Museum.}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15): Survey map reporting the kofun as square {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|WD=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (Shōwa 36): Residential development on the mound {{Sfn|堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|2014}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|WD=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} (Heisei 11): Excavations conducted by Sakai City Board of Education. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}} (Heisei 12): Excavations at TYM-2 site (reported in 2002). * {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20): Excavations at TYM-3 site (reported in 2010). * {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} (Heisei 22): Ground-penetrating radar survey and excavations at TYM-4 site (reported in 2012). * {{ill|2014|en|2014|WD=Q1999|lt=2014|1=2014}} (Heisei 26): Included in the Mozu Kofun Group National Historic Site {{Sfn|国cultural property等データベース|2014}}. * {{ill|2017|en|2017|WD=Q25290|lt=2017|1=2017}} (Heisei 29): Excavations at TYM-5 site (reported in 2018). * {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (Reiwa 1): Inscribed as a UNESCO World Historic Site site as part of the Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group. == Related Facilities == * '''Sakai City Museum''' (Sakai-ku, Mozu Akatsukiyama-cho): Displays burial goods from Terayama Minamiyama Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Sakai City. "Historic Site of Sakai: Mozu Kofun Group," 2014. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizuka Misanzai Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1797|Mozu Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/mozukofungun/terayamaminamiyama.html Terayama Minamiyama Kofun] - Sakai City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|寺山南山古墳|version=102006677|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q66124365|co|commons:Category:Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳}} [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
